lesson.menu_title: Presenting Yourself and Your Work -- Formal Self-Introduction and Institutional Pitch
lesson.menu_desc: The capstone of the bloco: present yourself and your institution, formally, in an international setting. Read a MODEL of a formal self-introduction in three moves -- who you are and your role, your experience and expertise, and a warm, forward-looking close -- and then make it your own for the plenary in Seoul. The class trains the four moves of a formal introduction (open and thank -> role and institution -> experience and achievement -> forward-looking close) and the phrases that carry them (It is an honor to be here. / I have worked in higher education for over twenty years. / I am looking forward to exchanging ideas with you.). Vocabulary: honored, expertise, partnership, opportunity, exchange, productive, contribution, collaboration. Expression recycled: 'I am looking forward to...' Idiom reactivated: 'I wear many hats.' -- 32 slides
lesson.subtitle: Lesson 20 -- Presenting Yourself and Your Work: Formal Self-Introduction
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Maria Clara | Lesson 20 -- Presenting Yourself and Your Work

listenings.text: Good afternoon, everyone. Thank you very much for having me here today. My name is Daniel, and I am a university professor and an institutional leader from Brazil. It is a real honor to be with you at this conference. I have worked in higher education for more than fifteen years, and my expertise is in international partnerships between universities. Over the years, I have represented my institution at meetings in many countries, and every single time, I learn something new. I am looking forward to exchanging ideas with all of you this week. I truly hope our conversations will lead to a productive partnership, and to a long collaboration between our institutions. Thank you again. I am very happy to be here, and I am happy to answer any questions.

Twenty lessons ago, I could not introduce myself in English. If someone asked me who I was, I went quiet, or I switched to Portuguese. Today is different. Today I can stand up at a conference in Seoul, look at the room, and say: good afternoon, my name is Maria Clara, and it is an honor to be here. I can say what I do. I can talk about my twenty years of experience, my two degrees, and my expertise. And I can say what I hope for: a productive partnership, an exchange of ideas, a real collaboration. My English is not perfect, and it does not need to be. It is calm, it is mine, and it is enough. After twenty lessons, I can present myself to the world, in English. And this time, I speak for my institution myself.

paras: Good afternoon, and thank you for having me here today. My name is Maria Clara, and it is a true honor to be with you. I am a professor and an institutional leader at a federal university in Maceió, Brazil. I am responsible for our international partnerships, and I represent my university when our delegation travels abroad.

I have worked in public administration and higher education for more than twenty years. I hold two doctoral degrees, and I have led teams and projects in several countries. My main expertise is in institutional leadership and international cooperation. For me, every conference is an opportunity to learn from other people and to make a small contribution of my own.

I am honored to be at this conference in Seoul. I am looking forward to exchanging ideas with all of you, and I truly hope this visit will be the beginning of a productive partnership between our institutions. I believe good things happen through collaboration, one honest conversation at a time. As we say back home, I wear many hats -- but today I am here, above all, to listen and to build something together with you. Thank you very much. I am very happy to answer any questions.
reading.source: Institutional & Travel English -- model introduction written for Maria Clara's package

reading.prompt: What is the main idea of this text?
choices: Maria Clara lists all the problems she had on her trip to Seoul.
Maria Clara introduces herself formally: who she is, her experience, and what she hopes for, building toward a partnership.
Maria Clara explains why she cannot attend the conference in Seoul.

items: Maria Clara works at a federal university in Brazil.
t
"I am a professor and an institutional leader at a federal university in Maceio, Brazil."
She has worked in her field for less than five years.
f
"I have worked in public administration and higher education for more than twenty years."
She holds two doctoral degrees.
t
"I hold two doctoral degrees..."
She thinks a conference is only a place to give orders, not to learn.
f
"every conference is an opportunity to learn from other people..."
She hopes the visit will lead to a partnership between the institutions.
t
"I truly hope this visit will be the beginning of a productive partnership between our institutions."

bank.label: Opening, presenting your role, sharing experience, and closing a formal introduction
items: Good afternoon. Thank you for having me here today.
My name is Maria Clara, and it is an honor to be with you.
I am a professor and institutional leader at a federal university in Brazil.
I have worked in higher education for more than twenty years.
I am looking forward to exchanging ideas with you.
I hope this visit will lead to a productive partnership.

items.situation: Open your introduction. Greet the room and thank them for the moment.
tips: Good afternoon. Thank you for having me here today.
It is a real honor to be with you.

items.situation: Say who you are and where you work -- your role and your institution.
tips: My name is Maria Clara, and I am a professor and institutional leader.
I work at a federal university in Maceio, Brazil.

items.situation: Share your experience in one sentence -- how long, and in what field.
tips: I have worked in higher education for more than twenty years.
My expertise is in institutional leadership.

items.situation: Say one thing you are proud of -- a real achievement or responsibility.
tips: I have led teams and projects in several countries.
I am responsible for our international partnerships.

items.situation: Say what you are hoping for at this conference -- look forward.
tips: I am looking forward to exchanging ideas with you.
I hope this visit will start a productive partnership.

items.situation: Close your introduction warmly and invite questions.
tips: Thank you very much. I am very happy to be here.
I am happy to answer any questions.

pressure.title: Answer live
items: In one sentence: who are you, and where do you work? Go.
You have thirty seconds. Give your whole introduction -- open, your role, your experience, and a warm close. Do not stop.
A rector at the Seoul plenary asks: 'What are you hoping to get out of this conference?' Answer with 'I am looking forward to...' and 'I hope...'. Go.

pressure.text: A formal self-introduction has four moves. OPEN and thank: 'Good afternoon. Thank you for having me.' ROLE and institution: 'I am a professor and institutional leader at...' EXPERIENCE and expertise: 'I have worked in... for over twenty years. My expertise is in...' Forward-looking CLOSE: 'I am looking forward to exchanging ideas.' / 'I hope this visit will lead to a productive partnership.' Keep it 60 to 90 seconds.
